title: Indian Food, Festivals and Culture
description: A lively round on Indian cuisine, celebrations, traditional arts, music, and cultural symbols.

questions.prompt: Which festival is known as the Festival of Lights?
questions.hint: It is celebrated with diyas and fireworks.

questions.prompt: Which harvest festival is celebrated with kites in Gujarat?
questions.hint: It takes place in January.

questions.prompt: Which festival is famous for colours and water balloons?
questions.hint: It marks the arrival of spring.

questions.prompt: Biryani is traditionally a dish made mainly with rice and what other main component?
questions.hint: It is usually meat or vegetables cooked with spices.

questions.prompt: Which South Indian dish is made from a fermented batter and served like a thin crisp crepe?
questions.hint: It is often paired with sambar and chutney.

questions.prompt: From which state is the dance form Kathakali associated?
questions.hint: It is on India's southwest coast.

questions.prompt: Which sweet is especially associated with Kolkata and is made from chhena?
questions.hint: It is white, soft, and soaked in syrup.

questions.prompt: Which Punjabi dish is commonly eaten with makki di roti?
questions.hint: It is made using mustard greens.

questions.prompt: Which festival marks the birth of Lord Krishna?
questions.hint: It often includes dahi handi celebrations.

questions.prompt: Which state is famous for the traditional dance Bihu?
questions.hint: It is a major northeastern state known for tea.

questions.prompt: What is the main ingredient of idli?
questions.hint: It is made from a fermented rice and lentil batter.

questions.prompt: Which festival celebrates the bond between brothers and sisters with a sacred thread?
questions.hint: It starts with the word Raksha.

questions.prompt: Which classical dance form originated in Tamil Nadu and is often performed in temple jewellery?
questions.hint: Its name starts with B.

questions.prompt: Puran Poli is a festive dish especially popular in which Indian state?
questions.hint: Its capital is Mumbai.

questions.prompt: Which festival is especially associated with the worship of Goddess Durga in West Bengal?
questions.hint: Large pandals are a major feature of it.

questions.prompt: What is lassi traditionally made from?
questions.hint: It is a dairy-based drink.

questions.prompt: Which city is especially famous for chaat and pani puri culture in Uttar Pradesh?
questions.hint: It is also known for the Kashi Vishwanath Temple.

questions.prompt: Which dance form from Odisha is known for graceful poses and temple-sculpture inspiration?
questions.hint: Its name begins with O.

questions.prompt: Which festival in Kerala is famous for boat races and a grand floral decoration called pookalam?
questions.hint: It is a harvest festival.

questions.prompt: What is the main filling of a traditional aloo paratha?
questions.hint: The clue is in the name.

questions.prompt: Which North Indian snack is a deep-fried pastry usually stuffed with spiced potato filling?
questions.hint: It is often triangular.

questions.prompt: Which sweet is commonly served during Ganesh Chaturthi and is said to be Lord Ganesha's favourite?
questions.hint: It has a coconut and jaggery filling.

questions.prompt: Which stringed instrument is strongly associated with Ravi Shankar?
questions.hint: It is used widely in Hindustani classical music.

questions.prompt: Which festival celebrates the Sikh New Year and the formation of the Khalsa?
questions.hint: It is widely celebrated in Punjab in April.

questions.prompt: Which festival marks the end of Ramadan for Muslims in India?
questions.hint: It is commonly called the smaller Eid.

questions.prompt: Which grand Rajasthani folk dance is performed by women balancing pots on their heads?
questions.hint: Its name starts with G.

questions.prompt: Which festival is famous in Tamil Nadu as a harvest thanksgiving celebration?
questions.hint: It is celebrated in January with sweet rice.

questions.prompt: What is the main lentil dish served with rice in many Indian homes and commonly called dal?
questions.hint: Its name is the same as the Hindi word for lentils.

questions.prompt: Which sweet dish is made by reducing milk and often flavoured with cardamom and nuts?
questions.hint: It is chilled and starts with K.

questions.prompt: Which festival is associated with the worship of Lord Ganesha on a large public scale in Maharashtra?
questions.hint: It ends with Chaturthi.

questions.prompt: Which dance form from Uttar Pradesh is associated with intricate footwork and storytelling?
questions.hint: It shares its name with one of India's classical dances beginning with K.

questions.prompt: Which traditional flatbread is baked in a tandoor?
questions.hint: It is common in North Indian restaurants.

questions.prompt: Which Gujarati snack made from gram flour is usually yellow and soft?
questions.hint: It is often eaten with green chutney.

questions.prompt: Which famous Hyderabadi dish combines rice, spices, and slow cooking?
questions.hint: It shares its name with a famous all-India rice dish.

questions.prompt: Which dance form from Andhra Pradesh features graceful expressions and storytelling?
questions.hint: Its name starts with K and ends with pudi.

questions.prompt: Which Maharashtrian street food consists of a spicy potato fritter inside a bun?
questions.hint: It is a Mumbai favourite.

questions.prompt: Which classical music tradition is associated mainly with North India?
questions.hint: It is paired with the Carnatic tradition in the south.

questions.prompt: Which classical music tradition is associated mainly with South India?
questions.hint: It is paired with the Hindustani tradition in the north.

questions.prompt: Which sweet from Rajasthan and many other states is a round syrup-soaked dessert often served hot?
questions.hint: Its name has two words and starts with G.

questions.prompt: Which festival in Assam celebrates the Assamese New Year and is also the name of a dance?
questions.hint: Its name starts with B.

questions.prompt: What is the main ingredient in paneer tikka besides spices?
questions.hint: It is Indian cottage cheese.

questions.prompt: Which state is especially associated with the dance form Garba during Navratri?
questions.hint: Its capital is Gandhinagar.

questions.prompt: Which festival is celebrated by Christians in India on 25 December?
questions.hint: It marks the birth of Jesus Christ.

questions.prompt: Which sweet made from gram flour, ghee, and sugar is called besan what?
questions.hint: It is a popular round festive sweet.

questions.prompt: Which beverage made with tea, milk, sugar, and spices is commonly called masala what?
questions.hint: It is a daily staple across India.

questions.prompt: Which snack from Delhi and North India is made from crisp puris, yogurt, chutneys, and spices?
questions.hint: Its name starts with Dahi.

questions.prompt: Which vegetarian Gujarati platter often includes multiple small dishes served together?
questions.hint: It is named after the style of serving itself.

questions.prompt: Which drum is commonly used in Punjabi bhangra performances?
questions.hint: It is a double-sided drum.

questions.prompt: Which sweet dish from Bengal is made from caramelised milk and is known for being soft and spongy?
questions.hint: Its name starts with M.

questions.prompt: Which colorful nine-night festival honors Goddess Durga and is famous for dandiya dances?
questions.hint: Its name starts with N.
